channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 2cars been left on road with flat tyres, 24th May
item.description:
The two vehicles CY58FOD and NG62MDE seem to have been left for at least 4 months and have not been moved and are now with flat tyres on both and are making the area look unsightly .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Raymere Gardens, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Please investigate

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Herne Hill Road, Herne Hill, Greater London

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nelldale Road, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Offensive graffiti on brown fence in alleyway, 24th May
item.description:
FixMyStreet ref: 9484647. Photo of this reportzoom Previously reported via Android in the Graffiti category anonymously at 23:21, Monday, FixMyStreet ref: 9473349 yet closed for being unable to find the grafitti in question. Maybe a closer look was required. Follow up: Still there or been replaced. I think still there and erroneously closed prematurely
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 289, Chipperfield Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bikes in disrepair, 24th May
item.description:
4 bikes chained to the bike rack for about 2 years now all showing signs of disrepair, wheels missing, one of them is just a frame and completely rusted over.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Helmsley Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Works unit causing vehicles to drive on pavement, 24th May
item.description:
A works unit has been parked on lamb walk for two weeks. It exceeds the width of the parking bays and subsequently traffic has to bypass on the opposite pavement. It is potentially a hazard for pedestrians. Today, a family with a pram were walking to their entrance door opposite the unit and had to reverse in order for the grocery van to pass them. Traffic should not be on the footpath!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Lamb Walk, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bin bag, 24th May
item.description:
Dumped bin bag next to bin
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bench still not fixed, 24th May
item.description:
The bench which was first reported months ago in this small park has still not been fixed.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge motorbike chain left on lamppost, 24th May
item.description:
A huge chain is left 24/7 attached to the lamppost, can cause an obstruction on pavement and/or road when motorbike isn’t there. Is it even legal to chain motorbikes to street furniture?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Heathfield Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked cars obstructing view of oncoming cars, 24th May
item.description:
There is a gap in the double yellows on Footscray Road which allows cars to park outside New Cutz barbers and Gogo boilers. There's often large vans and cars parked there which obstructs the view when pulling out of Hainault Street. Please investigate changing it to double yellows along that section and enforcing it to protect people, especially children, crossing the junction

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Footscray Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Thames water have been called but they have refused liability. There is also sand on the road so the water is becoming stagnant. This is unacceptable. Rubber was on road for nearly 4 weeks & parking is becoming more difficult as it already is normally., 24th May
item.description:
Thames water have been called but they have refused liability. There is also sand on the road so the water is becoming stagnant. This is unacceptable. Rubber was on road for nearly 4 weeks & parking is becoming more difficult as it already is normally.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Dorset Road, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
